hypothesis: Training and evaluating 3D molecular generators with a chemistry-grounded, valency-aware framework that uses Kekulized GEOM-Drugs data (narom–vother valency representation) will reduce generated-valency violations to below 1% and yield GFN2-xTB-relaxed geometries whose bond-length and bond-angle deviations from the initial generations are respectively below 0.01 Å and 0.5°, outperforming models trained under the original, non-Kekulized pipeline.
